meanings.definition: the act of marching; walking with regular steps (especially in a procession of some kind)
examples: we heard the sound of marching
They processed into the dining room
The Japanese marched their prisoners through Manchuria

meanings.definition: walk fast, with regular or measured steps; walk with a stride
examples: He marched into the classroom and announced the exam
The soldiers marched across the border

meanings.definition: march in protest; take part in a demonstration
examples: Thousands demonstrated against globalization during the meeting of the most powerful economic nations in Seattle
She parades her new husband around town

meanings.definition: cause to march or go at a marching pace
examples: They marched the mules into the desert

meanings.definition: lie adjacent to another or share a boundary
